text: Had accounts switched to ATT and T-Mobile continued to bill our credit card. Seemed like customer service took joy in not refunding the money. They got $560 of our families money on a technicality.
When you calls its says team of experts whereas in reality they have hired team of jerks. Just one agent to another and no solution.

We went to get our granddaughter's phone replaced seeing's January 1st 2022 her phone will not work on the new Network. I was talking to the employee telling her what I thought and she keep saying Shut up and I will explain. I Told her I was talking and she could explain when I Got done saying what I was saying. Her name is Cassie and she works for the T Mobile store in Clio Michigan. She slammed her I pad on the counter and told us to leave. That she wasn't waiting on us. She told us to go to another store if we wanted service. I said I will wait for the gentleman over there to get done and she said He isn't waiting on you either. I Asked who her supervisor is she said he was in the back and she assured me HE wouldn't wait on me either. So We ended up going to the store in Flint Michigan and We couldn't have asked for better service. HOW can a Company allow there employees to treat paying customers like this. What is this world coming to. When I worked Retail , If I treated anyone like she treated us I Would have lost my Job.. BUT OH WAIT with  COVID nobody want's to work so they allow employees to treat customers in this way and they get away with it because NOBODY else want's to work.  ON our way out of the store , this employee had the nerve to say I LOVE YOU...NOW MIND YOU SHE REFUSED TO WAIT ON US THEN SAYS TO US heading out the door I love you.  What does that tell you  about that employee??? Tells me a lot , she isn't right in the head.

We have 55+ senior plan T Mobile with 2 year contract on the Galaxy S20 FE expires 10/2022. I called yesterday to get a quote for two Catholic priests, neither are ages 55 and over. The representative  gave a quote for essential unlimited for $50. But she hang up on me. I called the superior of the 2 priest and shared the quote. I called again and the representative quoted me for $90 for 2 phones. And she said the first quote was not right. I asked a quote again through the website -chat with an expert- ( And I asked for a live person not a robot) I got Ny. I asked a quote for a non member of T mobile. I asked a quote for affordable plan for 2 phones and he quoted me for $70. I copy and paste his quote.
......"the Magenta plan is the lowest plan unlimited with taxes included for 2 lines on autopsy $70
do you want a tax-included plan. The essential is not taxes included".............
I new the quote is for the senior plan. And I responded, there is no age 55+ among us. And he said yes that is for the senior plan. And I respond, why did you give a quote for senior plan, you did not asked about our age? And finally I type. "You guys are just after your commission or for your benefit". I ended the conversation. I try to recommend T mobile to the religious organization. But will definitely not recommend.  I called the T mobile again and ask when our contract for the phone expires and how much we owe if we terminate our contract. If we terminate now we owe $641 since our contract is until 10/2022. 
CAN T MOBILE JUST TRAIN YOUR CUSTOMER REPRESENTATIVES?

I have never dealt with a more pathetic customer support department.  One to two hour holds.  No call back when promised. When contact is made, the person speaks poorly and is unable to solve the issue.  I tried their IT service for one month, multiple disconnects and only got charged about a $100 for nothing.  Don’t even think about doing business with them.
DO NOT switch to T Mobile. Their customer service is the worst. They make promises on things (bills, cell phones) as well as retuned calls that do not happen. Biggest mistake we made.
Terrible service! The worst I have ever had! Even walmart service was 100 times faster! This was the worst company I have ever had the displeasure of dealing with!!!!!?!!

I was an early adopter of T-Mobile's home internet service. Great speeds early on in the range they currently claim of 80-110mbps. However, for the last 9 months my speeds have steadily declined due to them overselling their capacity. My last call was a few days ago with their solution being to give me priority on the tower that is approx 4 blocks from my house. Did it work? My speed today is .87mbps. Yes, less than 1mbps! Don't waste your time with T-Mobile's home internet. They have a good support team but there's nothing they can do for you until T-mobile invests in more capacity.

Horrible customer service. I've had my phone since June of 2021 and they do not show one call text or one gigabyte used of data. They offer no explanation and just keep transferring me and starting me over and over and over I can't stand this company
The service is very bad they take advantage of people I will never recommend the service to anyone !!!!

On Sep. 23rd, I went to my local T-Mobile to upgrade my phone to the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 3. 512G. This is my experience....

My local T-Mobile store had a promo to upgrade from my Note 10+ to the Z fold 3 for a $1000 trade in. GREAT DEAL!!! I had to pay off my Note 10+ which was $116. I also had to upgrade my line to Unlimited Plus, $10 more monthly. I also paid $30 to upgrade, and $235 for taxes on the new phone. I paid just south of $400 after all was said and done. I have a receipt that they emailed me that proves it. I left with an installment agreement in place and a delivery date of 3-4 weeks.

It is now Nov. 8th and I do not have the phone, nor is there a delivery date anywhere in sight. This is the best information that I can get after going to the store 2 times, talking with the store 3 times, chatting with CS 2 times, and talking to CS on the phone 3 times. I have spent a total of 9+ hours trying to get information regarding ETA. 

The main issue is, (BUYER BEWARE) after the purchase is made at the store, the info is sent to shipping. When the item is "picked" and it is time to ship the phone, an order number is created. When the phone is actually shipped, the customer receives an email with an order number so as to track the shipment. 

Until the order is shipped, NO ORDER NUMBER EXISTS. This means, no one, and I mean NO ONE has any information regarding the order. So, now, I have been waiting since Sep 23rd and as far as T-Mobile (store, chat, and CS by phone) goes, there is LITERALLY no way to track the order. It lives in limbo. No one can help me. I have been a human ping-pong ball for the last 3 weeks, trying to figure out when or even IF I will receive the phone.  CS by phone had a hard time even finding any information at all about the phone. Chat never found information. The store seemed a slightly better alternative.

Today, I decided that I am done dealing with this. I just want to get a refund and be done. No. Not happening. It is impossible to refund an order that doesn't exist. Except, it does exist since I paid money, have a store receipt, and even have an installment agreement number. 

I spent 48 minutes on the line with customer service this evening, and after waiting on hold while the rep tried to figure out whether or not I had purchased a phone at all , I was hung up on. Yes. Hung up on. Maybe accidentally, most likely deliberately since we both were getting pretty frustrated. I received no call back. I called them after spending 24 minutes on the phone with my local store who pinged, or is it ponged? me to them. After waiting 10 minutes for CS to call me back (surely we were accidentally disconnected, right?) I decided to call the store again. This resulted in a 20 minute conversation with an employee who informed me that I could not cancel until the phone ships. You know, because I do not have an order number. 

This story has many, many details that I would love to share, but to avoid this becoming an ACTUAL novel, I chose to put the most relevant info in this message. 

The juicy stuff, those details, make me want to throw a brick through my local store's window. But, I won't. Instead, maybe I should file for a paycheck for the many hours of being ping-ponged. And, maybe I should file an expense report for my mileage. Ha Ha. Loyalty only flows one way with this company, and I assure you, the customer is not on the receiving end. 

So, here I am, writing this and basically copying and pasting it to every single review site I can find because I have no other recourse. I cannot cancel the phone that may never come. I cannot get my money back because I cannot cancel. I cannot get a different phone because I have a lease for a phone that I don't have possession of. I am literally in limbo. And, they don't care. Not even a little as has been evidenced by the many calls, chats, and in-person visits that have gotten me nowhere. 

Apparently, there is a black hole that exists in their universe. Your money, your time, your inability to move forward, are all sucked in. And, T-Mobile has no idea how to find it. Literally no idea. Who knows, I may not even have a phone on order. They cannot tell me I do because there is no order number.  What if I wait 3 more months and no phone comes. Will they be able to refund me then? No. I don't have an order number so you cannot cancel something in limbo apparently.

I asked how long I should have to wait. I was told however long it takes - because there is a chip shortage. Well no da Sherlock. I get it. But, now that I know that, 7 weeks later, 3-4 weeks longer than I was told, I would just appreciate my money back and the phone "order" canceled. But, there is no order number. Therefore, there is no order. Therefore, I cannot get my money back. I guess I will remain in purgatory. Maybe for 2 weeks, or 2 months, or 2 years. Who knows, they sure as hell don't.
I am a sprint customer. I left T mobile because of the horrible experience that I had.

I was with sprint when all of a sudden tmobile took over. I was paying full insurance and paying for 3 phones a month.l, paying 300 plus a month,, Well I had a tiny crack in my screen and my phone suddenly stops responding and I called sprints insurance....its not even a thing anymore. And there's no sprints to call, so I call tmobile and their ***holes to me, not helping me, saying my policy is not found and there is nothing they can do. I call 4 times...finally someone says okay we can try to help you....trys to file a claim. Takes my debit visa info and charges me 100 dollars to fix my phone and says I'll have a package in 2 days to send you a new phone......a week later and nothing. I call and appear a file was not claimed ......I was absolutely pissed.  I hate that I'm even in this position and have to go this this bulli***, and transition of the 2. This is the absolute worst service I've ever experienced in my life. And I still can't believe I had to pay out of pocket for this ****. Thanks a whole lot for nothing tmobile...**** you.

My husband and, I was customers of Metro PCS, the plans were good, but buying phones out right very expensive, so on August 24, 2021, we decided to get new phones and get on a contract plan and choose T-Mobile, I will not put location, but they're located in the state of ( Indiana). We really had our minds set on the Notes 20, but, T-Mobile was pushing the iphone buy one get one free, the Note wasn't like that, then the sales person showed us the ( revvl V+ 5g) again we really wanted the Note but the pricing was expensive $1200 a pop and we needed phone like STAT. 
So the sales person talked us into the revvl V+ 5g phones, the phones were good while in the store, in the car all the way home. Then issues came, with my phone it started with the messaging, I wasn't receiving  any notifications, I would have to just go to message box on my own to see, if someone sent me a message, this keep going a few days. I contacted customer service spoken with representative, they took me through steps needed and even reset phone, we thought issue was taken care of problem still occurring.
I have been speaking with someone at T-Mobile from 9/27/21-11/5/21, I have appreciated credits in which a customer representative gave me from Colorado, but she didn't explain to me that the credit needed to go towards the bill, and I couldn't use it the way, I wanted to use it. This same customer service representative also, stated to me on Monday, November 1, 2021 at 347pm. ( Her exact words was; looking at the promotion we have going on we do have the buy one get one  on us with a new line, would you like this since you just activated a line? you would qualify for this promotion. Then she go on to say, the current devices that we have will continue until the balance is paid off does this make sense and we were chatting via Twitter all the way until Wednesday( November 2, 2021) then she stated on Tuesday @ 1052 am that a deposit was needed of $449.20, she claimed, she didnt see this due to our balance of $32 50?? really. why would you honor  saying we can get the Notes, then change it, plus the phones still messed up, my phone connects to WIFI, when, I have 5g, but it wont work when it's not connected with 5g, and then T-mobile states their tower connection in Merrillville, IN is in good range and connection is great, but even a technical support agent, already stated that t-mobile merged with Sprint and many customers having issues with phones, but we have been having this issue for two months, then t-mobile said we can use our warranty, but get this we have  to pay a stock fee, but we got that waived, then we asked, were the phones going to be new? The representative stated she dont know what's in warehouse??That's a red flag for me, first of all why should we use our warranty, when it's t-mobile fault, then they changed me and my husband sim card and that did nothing, but a t-mobile employee told me that she turn her phone on airplane mode when she's at home, because she gets bad service. I said wait a minute. I said you pay over a hundred something dollars and you put your phone on airplane mode> I said the DEVIL is a lair.. NO NO HELL, HELL NO. We pay over $ 120 a month this issue will be corrected it's unethical, unappreciated, and down right disrespectful, I have supervisor whom wanted to help me, but no one reaches back, spoken with multiple customer service representatives and they state, well the customer service agent from Twitter will be in contact after November 12, 2021. I have all Twitter chats as well as chats from T-mobile site. We just want t-mobile to honor the Notes 20 that was promised to use, no deposit of $449.20 when you  know we owe over $300 for phone or take phones as trade- ins, but this issue needs to be settled, because it's been an inconvenience for over two months now looking for some closure

I can't speak for other locations but for me, the T-Mobile store located at 4825 Stone Mountain Hwy, Lilburn, GA has gone beyond my expectation with setting things right, communicating what's happening during each step of the process, and providing the necessary follow-up when there is a delay. Perhaps this store and its employees are the templates that should be duplicated.

Two hours of Torture. We tried at the T-Mobile store in DAnvers ma, to switch from sprint to t-mobile. After standing there with the salesperson gor TWO hours, we were told “there is a system error” and “it’s been going on since yesterday” - I’m not going back to the store and I’m switching to Att or Verizon. We asked to speak with a manager but he was out in the back-avoiding accountability.
Continuous lies and I'm tired of it they basically explained that it was a shared network and what I'm paying for I need to share with my neighbor so my $400 for my network is shared with my neighbor

DON'T EVEN GIVE THEM ANOTHER THOUGHT! If there was a rating below one STAR I would give it.  This is the worst cell phone company I have every used.  They do not disclose all of the hidden fees with their promotions--you will be surprised when you start getting your bill!  We signed up with them in December 2020 and have been trying to get out of our contract since February 2021.  The cell phone service is horrible and we live in one of the most populated areas in our city--and we can see the cell phone tower from our back patio.  In fact, we usually have to go outside of the house to make a phone call. Beware, they program their phones where you can't transfer them to other carriers without more than a considerable amount of time and effort.  I am past being aggravated and angry. I am distraught. Please read this review and take my sincere advice--DO NOT CONSIDER T-MOBILE.

Horrible and disgusting! No service and was promised 5G. Got 2 miles from my house and could not make a call or text. Had the service for 5 days and they ripped me off for a HUGE amount of money. DO NOT and I REPEAT...............DO NOT EVER PAY THEM AHEAD ! They STEAL YOUR MONEY!!!!!!!!!!!!

TMobile has been the worst and the most traumatic experience I have ever had service wise. I initiated a warranty exchange, they told me everything was good and that they received the old phone returned and everything was successfully completed with the exchange. Next thing I know I have a $500 charge on my account 3 months later! I called and they said it was for the warranty exchange from 3 months ago! I spent days and hours on the phone with them so they could fix the issue as they had already assured me that the exchange was successfully completed 3 months ago. The representatives are all liers, which means the company is a huge scam. They kept lying to get me off the phone, passing me around from person to person, while doing nothing to resolve the issue. I lost DAYSS! not hours, DAYS! Run away from this place, I have left as well. Also, all their phones are trash and always break a few months in, I'm done with them for good.

I am a T Mobile cellular customer who tried to get home internet.  I checked online and my address shows I am eligible for internet with their new service. I called and got someone in the Philippines who said NO that my address showed not available. I checked again and online the address checker showed I was clearly eligible for this service. T Mobile has a bait and switch advertisement that lets you think you are signing up for the internet but in truth, they are only collecting your SS number and other info with an overseas company employee. Their internet ad is a scam.
Tmobile is filtering/blocking my incoming text.   What gives me the right!

I have had had t mobile for 6 years, i recently switched over to my sister in laws plan who had sprint which is now t mobile. Can i tell you how pissed off i am with them now....my brand new iPhone 12 pro max randomly shut off and didn't turn back on two weeks after I got it. went to t mobile to see if they could help and of course they couldn't help me they brushed me off and told me to go to apple and they couldn't give me a new one because they didn't have anymore which is BS you a phone store and you had no phones at all? every time I call there customer service number they are F***** use less i have to call multiple times to get someone helpful. the sad things i have had every phone service and this one is the cheapest so only reason I keep it!

The 4G service has really declined over the last few months after they focused more on setting up 5G in my area. I have an old iphone 7 and don't have 5G. My 4G LTE data is sooooo sloooooooow it's rediculous. Now I have to get a new $1000 phone in order to get decent data.

I have been with T-Mobile for over 15 years, and been happy with them. But last month while in the store, I was talked into taking a great promotion where they would give me $800 for my current phone and I could get an ungraded phone for $800.... so.... it would be free for me! I didn't really need a new phone but decided to take the upgrade since it was free. I repeatedly asked the associate why they would be doing a "free" promotion and she convinced me that I qualified for it...  I would just need to send in my old phone to complete it. Well fast forward 6 weeks.....already sent in my old phone... well over 8 phone calls to T-Mobile, hours on the phone, wasted time waiting for the store manager or customer service to call back.... and they say I am not ELIGBLE for the promotion. That I don't have the right rate plan. They refuse to honor what I was promised. I am so frustrated with T-Mobile, and cant wait till we can cancel our 7 lines with them. It is not my fault that the store associate messed up.... she told me I qualified, so I did took the promotion.  Shame on T-Mobile for not standing behind what they promised.  We will take our 7 lines to a company that can be trusted!

T Mobile sells the fastest 5G network but really its the same old crappy Sprint.  I have been a customer for several years I have a 5G phone but I do not get 5G speed.  When you talk to tech support they lie and will tell you anything.  The truth is T Mobile has a network that is congested and unable to fully support 5G.  This is known as DECEPTIVE TRADE.  T Mobile should be stripped of the merger with Sprint.  It did not make for a better customer experience.  It just allowed Sprint to hide from all the litigation.

WORST SERVICE EVER!!! I had sprint before they merged for 4 YEARS WITH NO ISSUES!! Then had to switch my Sims card to t-mobile and BAM HORRIBLE HORRIBLE SERVICE. phone calls constantly drop, my phone always says not connected to a network and the "solution" the gentleman in the store told me to just use wifi for my calls. FIRST OFF I shouldn't HAVE to have wifi for a service I am PAYING FOR!! I am always missing important calls because of this issue. I've tried to be patient while they " get more towers" but it's been 3 months of this nonsense! I can not believe sprint ruined such a great service by merging with such  A HORRIBLE service.

I wish I could give 0 stars. I was hoping when tmoble took over sprint things would get better, but they got worse. I provide a phone for mother who lives in another state. She had to upgrade because of the 3g going away and she had an S7. This is already hard for her to learn a new phone. So I buy her an S20 FE cash and have it sent to her address. Let her know she can go to the store and they will help her with everything. Well, they won't. She has to figure out how to do it on her own. The reason is because it is a sprint phone and all the sprint stores are T-moble now. What BS.  And knowing the CS reps I have talked to on the phone this will be a nightmare for her. I AM DISCUSTED with this so called CS. This was just the last straw in a long line of being lied to and misled about plans etc. I will be canceling service as soon as it suits me to do so (stuck in a buy one get one trap) I will also be posting more on any social media app I can as T-mobile obviously doesn't give a care about its customers. Take my advise. Verizon is the way to go. Even their pricing lately is comparable.

Is it the best internet...No
Is it the fastest....No
But I find it to be very reliable and fast enough for my needs.
It's reasonably priced, has no data caps and you don't need a land line to get it. Which in an area prone to bad storms and downed lines is important.
I'm also able to stream multiple televisions with no problems so I've been able to cut the cable and halve my internet and television bills. (I now use youtube tv instead of comcast)

When I've had to call customer service, they've always been very helpful and diligent in solving any problems I have had. They've also been very open and admitted if the problem was on their side and not tried to blame me like some internet companies I know.

So overall I give them 5 stars. They're just starting out as a home internet provider and I think they're doing a great job. Like anyone just starting out, they're not perfect. They are, however, determined to give you the best internet possible.
T-Mobile is a piece of crap. T-Mobile sucks.

Since T-Mobile took over Sprint it's a cluster fracking.
We are Sprint Legacy customers but that means nothing to T-Mobile, they do not recognize it. We were also told that the Legacy program ends December 31,2021 at which time we will (supposedly)be T-Mobile customers (I thought we already were) we are but only on paper...WHAT?!?!. They buy accounts from other phone companies BUT NOT Sprint....WHAT!?!?. In order for us to switch to T-mobile we must pay off our phones and then they'll be free to screw us over under the full T-Mobile name. We have been loyal customers of Sprint for over 11 years but they did nothing to protect their customers.  Time to find a new phone company.

Stay away at all costs what seems like a savings will cost you a fortune bought a new phone didn't work from day one they replaced it with a refurbished phone the phone is a piece of garbage and so is t-mobile

They have poor coverage.  I have to use my wifi because their cell service is so slow even with 5g.  I waited an hour and a half just to get a Sim card at their store.   I loved my sprint coverage.  T-mobile is spottier and slower.  Also I hate pink, what a stupid color and now my phone is all badged in the ugly pink t-mobile and I don't have a choice.  I recommend looking elsewhere for good cell  service and coverage.   So sad to say goodbye to Sprint.

T mobile internet service would be good if you could actually use it but it always disconnects you also the ping is always over 100 so don't plan on playing any games. Till they fix the service don't get it. Called 3 times and all they tell me is the towers are being worked on.

I have an iPhone 8 Plus that I purchased from T-Mobile several years back it is paid off I’ve been having cover issues where I live or T-Mobile so upon shopping for options with different companies I noticed that my phone was locked so I called T-Mobile to ask them to unlock it because I own the phone now it’s been paid off for a while so I called customer service T-Mobile and I believe in my heart that they try to scam me from the minute I told him to unlock my phone because the first person I spoke to asked me why I want it unlocked and I made the mistake by letting them know that I was considering moving on from their company which caused a chain reaction on their part to con and trick me of unlocking my phone they sent me to a specialist after I spent the next 20 minutes with the first person saying that it’s unlocked on their side but I kept telling her that I’m looking at my phone settings under general then you go to about and then you scroll down at the bottom and my phone still said lock so she gave me a specialist which that person also badgered me about why I want my phone unlocked and I got to the point where I was very upset at this point I just told her it’s my phone I own it unlocked the darn thing and the reason why am writing this review is because they try to trick me they sent me a link for me to do a factory reset on my phone so that I would lose everything I have in there because it to me in my opinion if they were going to lose me as a customer they were going to help me mysteriously to lose all my contacts and all my all my information on my phone so the last person told me I had to do it I factory reset an order to get my phone unlocked which is a lie so I just agreed that I would do that but I was not going to I told them I was going to go to one of the stores in person and get my phone unlocked and after I hung up because they knew how angry I was spending 40-45 minutes on the phone to try to unlock my phone which was my right it was my phone I owned it out right soon as I hung up I checked my phone and long behold it was unlocked so they spent 40-45 minutes lying to me to discourage me from unlocking my phone in their fear of me going somewhere else I don’t trust this company anymore after my experience and that’s my opinion but I needed to put it out there so that somebody else that is considering leaving T-Mobile and going to another company would know the trickery‘s and the dishonesty that I was subject to it’s funny I had no contracts with them and they brag about it all the time but when it comes to considering changing or changing to another company they’re gonna make you jump through hoops and you would think you had a contract by the way you are treated that’s my opinion thank you

Cant give 0 stars.  They said they would do a "soft credit check" when I owned the phones.  My credit went down 30 points.  Spoke to the owner and he said nothing he could do, I had to dispute it with the credit company.  Over 1 yr later, I chose to go with another carrier.  9 months later after I paid the final bill, they extorted another 63.75 from me that went to collections affecting my credit again.  I could never get an answer as to why I owed them the money 9 months later.  Glad to be rid of them.

Honestly, T-Mobile has been a great service ever since I switched over to them from Sprint but their "360 Protection" plan is an absolute scam and complete waste of money. I was paying $13 a month for a year a half after after I broke my previous phone and figured why not get insurance so the process is simpler and I can avoid having to pay full price again for a new phone. My current phone finally breaks and I find out that my $350 phone (now not even worth $120 brand new) won't be replaced without paying a $100 deductible. I could understand $100 maybe if I had a flagship phone but this was just insane considering I found my phone refurbished and like new selling online for around the same price.

Long story short, do NOT get the phone insurance unless maybe you have a really expensive phone, and even still, I can see them still charging a ridiculous deductible that almost negates having the insurance to begin with. I mean, even with my middle range phone the deductible was more than it cost to just get another phone online. So yeah, do the math, $13 x 18 months = $234 that could have bought me the brand new version of my current phone where T-Mobile is simply giving me a pre-owned refurbished phone and STILL charging me $100 for the replacement, so really, it's costing me $324 to get a crumby replacement when I could easily buy an even better phone than my current one, brand new.

So yeah, I give T-Mobile 3 stars because I love everything else about them but their insurance is just an awful scam. Too many hoops to jump through and then they'll still charge you through the nose just to get an even worse version (refurbished) of your current phone. Assurant is the company they use for their phone insurance. I know my rage should be focused solely on Assurant, but T-Mobile did decide to use them, so they still get a lot of the blame.

I had bought a iphone12 in July with BangO plan ( buy one and get one free). With the account set up and split out. The bond is broken for the new line. I have called customer service and gone back to the local store several times . After 3 months, the problem is still not solved.
Worst customer service.

I canceled my service back in August 2021, after a month of cancelation t-mobile pull a payment from the account where my autpay was set, I have been calling every week and went to the stores 5 times for my person verification to get my refund and still nothing, the customer service keeps telling me it is a refund being process in 2 to 3 days, still nothing. I wish it was an agency that can protect you from companies like this one
Never use t mobile. Not only they have terrible connections but also a horrible customer service department. Save your money and never deal with this company.

I've been having issues with LG Q7 plus phone were there manufactured issues and I was promised a brand new phone un refurbished just to get a refurbished phone I called to let them know that I was promised this and they said okay we're going to give you a free upgrade I got do not want to do it right now I just reprogram this phone manually so they left a note on my account so once this phone failed yet again I could call in and switch it out so I went and did that and they said that offer is no longer available at the phone that they're offering me and they gave me this other phone which was a T-Mobile manufacturer phone and it was crappy I believe it was called The revvl 5G Plus do not ever buy so I sent the new one back and still kept my broken down LG Q7 phone and trying to get T-Mobile to stand behind their promise of giving me a phone at no cost to me is very impossible they say I keep on calling and on this issue while I have spoken to someone on October 25th who said they're going to send me a certain type of phone and a new SIM card and will call me in a certain amount of days to help me set up the phone never got it finally found out that there is giving me a credit monthly and take for me to keep on using broken down LG Q7 plus phone where the battery does not hold its charge so they really messed up my account and they're denying me the right to speak to the supervisor. And I have been with T-Mobile for at least a decade most the time I was under someone else's account who I was the one that advised them to go T-Mobile and then I took over I've been very dissatisfied with this company in the last month wanted me to leave them.

I have been with T-Mobile for over 15 years, my credit is perfect, always paid my bill on time, etc. I recently moved to an area where T-Mobile service does not work so was forced to go to another provider. I called T-Mobile  notifying them my situation and let them know I was going to pay my final payment when the final bill was made available (which I was at this point forced to call with payment info, they automatically deleted my access to pay online). I paid this last week, and today I received a notice stating they placed it in collections...it wasn't even 30 days, not sure how that is even legal or legit, but I was a devoted customer, and this is how they treat their long term customers. SICKENING, and will NEVER go back to T-Mobile again after this!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

I was with sprint once they merged mobile is a horrible company. They do not value their customers at all. I see this company is all about the money but does not care about their customers. Not valuing your customer that pays you will force them to leave. Tmobile needs to look into valuing its customers first.

I switched to T-mobile and was excited to try this service but it was not all it cracked up to be. I was not able to get phone calls or text messages and went to the store replaced the SIM card, called customer service several times but they could not help me. I could only receive texts from other t-mobile customers. I ended up going back to Verizon because I am in a job that requires me to be available and needed to be able to get calls or texts. I switched back with in a week and it cost me $240. If you do go this route please demand to talk to IT and then maybe they will refund your money. This was extremely disappointing and the customer support was less than stellar. The only nice thing they did was tell me nicely they are keeping my money.

So TMobile gave me a great deal. 3 lines for 90.00 plus the cost of the phone and accessories for 135.00 including tax 

Until I get the first bill 

Turns out to be 188.00

So I call the 611 and they tell me who told me the bill would be 135.00? So I conference call the store manager and the 611 people together to hash it out and they disagree on about every detail but after twisting and turning they agree to give me a bill for 140.00. So  I figure I’m still saving money so I agree. Two days later I check the bill online but no changes….so I call again. Another rep says let me look into it. Says not sure how they got 140.00 the true bill is 143.00 so again I say ok but please adjust the first bill so all the credits are applied. No problem Sir I’m doing it right now. A day later I check the bill online again and no changes 

So this time I chat online and get another rep and he’s the best but says there’s no mention of any of this in my account but says no problem I’ll issue the credit immediately and he does and he tells me going forward your bill is 135.00. Perfect 

The next day I get a call from the TMobile  supervisor that wants to explain what my bill really is ….he says no Sir 135.00 no can do your bill is 159.00. With that I conference call the District Manager and try to hash it out 
They can’t or won’t so I tell him I want to meet them in the store next week because this is absurd 

So the next day I call and they say Sir let me give you an exact breakdown it’s 149.00. 

So lets recap no changes in anything 

135.00
188.00
140.00
143.00
135.00
159.00
149.00

I can’t believe I’m actually dealing with a giant telecommunication company 

At one point I said if I don’t get the bill resolved and at 135.00 I am going back to Verizon 

The rep interpreted that as a request to terminate my service and then demanded I pay the full price for the new phone 
That lasted about 2 mins before they reinstated my service and got back to the billing issue 

This is the most incredibly bad experience I’ve ever had and I have the patience of Jobe but this one takes the cake 

Don’t believe anything the store people tell you because they either don’t know the 20 variations of programs or what I believe is the billing dept decides how they want to bill you no matter how the store codes your account 

A complete _ _ _ _ show 

Very sad

I spent hours on the phone with T-Mobile reps to clarify the details of switching to their service and buying 3 phones. the end result was +$400 for the phones and +$500 for the service plans (over the life of the contact). I can't tell you how long we spent discussing the specifics, so I was furious that the deal proposed was not honored. The company makes the transaction extremely complicated so you never can pin down exactly what you signed up for. It is a scam.

THE WORST CUSTOMER SERVICE ON THE PLANET.  I opened a business account 4 weeks ago and had innumerable coverage issues at my home address.  Prior to signing up with T-Mobile, their in-store representative showed me a map of the area surrounding my home and it indicated that I would have 5-bar coverage.  WRONG.  I called T-Mobile tech support and one of their representatives actually told me to change to another carrier because there was a small area around my address where T-Mobile COULD NOT provide service.  Over the next several days I was at the store that signed me up and while they tried to helpful, they also had to call their customer service department to request assistance.  I spent 4 hours at the store with the in-store sales agent ON HOLD waiting for a representative to answer. The T-Mobile phone representative had NO CLUE what they were doing and I had to call back a day later.  I was disconnected at least 6 times over the next several days, sometimes after having spent :45 minutes or more on the phone with them - mostly on hold.  Of course, no one ever called me back.  Finally, after more than 15 excruciating hours of frustration on multiple calls, a supervisor authorized a refund on my account, but I was told I had to call another number the next day to request the refund.  The next day I spoke to another supervisor at the number I was given the day before.  I was told that the information I was given about a refund by the first supervisor was absolutely NOT POSSIBLE.  The -
T-Mobile phone customer service personnel are overseas and next to impossible to communicate with coherently.  I am canceling service with T-Mobile and switching to another carrier ASAP.  THE WORST EVER CUSTOMER SERVICE.  AVOID T-MOBILE at all cost.
There is never service. Calls always drop and you can’t get on the internet unless your on WIFI. Do not switch to their service

Worst phone company ever! My phone bill is 30 more than quoted. When I went to the company run store he said I was Mis quoted. Reception is horrible. They have no reception within 5 miles of where I purchased the phone was not informed of when I changed the carrier 
Was told I got free coverage due to a data breach. Billed an additional $10 a month now

Stay away from T-Mobile 

#t-mobilesucks#

Customer service is 100% joke and can’t provide any assistance or even answer questions.
Absolute waste of time to call customer service or use online chat.
After 12 years with Sprint we are moving to another carrier.

A year  ago I bought a new phone from T-mobile in Ferndale, Wa.  The T-mobile representative at that store put me on a new plan without my consent. My bill went from $109 to $159 for a whole year or more. When I contacted T-Mobile to ask why this was happening, they couldn't tell me why or who changed it but they could give me a little credit.
I have been with this company for over 20 years, very disappointed in their Customer Care Management and how you train your store level representatives.
Shame on me for trusting T-Mobile, definitely looking at other cellular carriers.

Service and Signal just get worse and worse!  I have NEVE4 had 4G appear on my IPhone 6 and 11, lucky to get two bars!  Now the want to do away with 3G - that will kill my phone!  Their answer, use your internet providers WiFi instead!  Going to Pure Talk in 10 days!

We were promised by store staff in the Ferndale department that when we upgraded our plan to the magenta max that our 3rd line was free.

This turned out to be false and customer service refused to correct the problem. Also the didn’t give me a eligible trade in for my phone and several other problems that we had to deal with. All in all we’ve spent about 12 hrs in the store and another 3 hrs on the phone trying to rectify these problems and as of now is till not solved.

T-Mobile used to have great customer service. Since they bought Sprint, it's been downhill. Their so-called experts are far from experts and they always appear to be busy reading their flowcharts. Spectrum looks better by the day.

Tmobile internet is the WORST EVER, as soon as I can speak to an Agent I'm dropping this mess. I wouldn't recommend this to anyone. It's a rip-off and you can only speak to some none speaking English people. Never again!!!!!
T-mobile forced us to update our phones to the 5G network. Wait until the last minute to switch over becuase it SUCKS right now!!

Where do I began purchased the z fold 2  4 mos ago cost $1800 not a problem  nice phone  but  on the Oct  27 today is Oct 29 I open my phone or had a line in it so you no I'm going then it in get them to fix it but I decided to just get the upgrade $1100 the z fold 3 as if that's not enough 2 day delivery was $25 do you know today  Oct 29 i called they haven't even shipped  the phone even after sending  me an e mail that says it had so she tells me the phone will be sent out on Nov 1 or I can get my money back on my card I want my phone not  the money  I MEAN THAT IS SAD SAD SAD

Sprint was great for years & then T-Mobile took over & it has been a total nightmare ever since. Poor cell reception service, an awful phone that was recommended by them, poor service with representatives who can't find a solution to my issues. Why did they have to ruin my phone service with this merger that can't merge?

I needed a phone because of my heart issue...had Sprint before and had good service. Purchased a flip phone and it did not work well in my area. Went to t-mobile to find another phone to replace the one that did not work. I bought a FLAT phone...could not figure out how to use it. Returned it and lost $150...I am on SS only and that is two weeks of groceries. Not happy with that company!!!!!!

T-Mobile started out good, but over the past five days, T-Mobile has proven to have a culture of lying. Very disappointed, especially with a supervisor once I got a hold of one, very run and condescending. I was told on three different occasions that a defective Apple watch would be replaced. Support continually told me that they would follow up with text and email to confirm the replacement. Never received text or email. Finally, after about the 20th call to T-Mobile, the support person told me to go to any T-Mobile store for a replacement. The next morning I went to a T-Mobile store, which opened 15 minutes late, then waited another hour before telling me that no replacement was going through. It just got worse from that point.

T mobile isn't a very good company. You call 500 times before you actually get any help. You have to go in to get a replacement phone. Asked numerous people how to get a virus off your phone. No one had a clue.. They can't wait for you to leave too. Sure miss good customer service especially when you pay a fortune for a bill.
One of the worst customers service I have ever gotten in my life. Spend 5+ hour on resolving billing issue where they are over charging me. In store and corp rep says different thing each time.

If negative stars was an option that's what I would select.
Slow and highly inconsistent! But never even close to good.

2 SIM cards later Seriously they can’t do a simple thing to switch from post paid to prepaid took me hours trying to explain what I wanted they just keep saying I need a new SIM card!! I’m done with T-Mobile!!!

Their website is down about 80% of the time for the Tmobile business account. You basically have to get lucky when you want to review all of your lines and pick the right time when their servers are actually working. Anytime you have a technical support question, you get transferred to a person that's still using a flip phone from the 90s and has no idea how to even work a basic smartphone and are absolutely oblivious to technical details without reading through a manuscript.

If you happen to get their POOR home internet be sure to make sure your bill is paid in full.. They WILL NOT send you a final bill in the mail or via your registered email. However, they WILL send you to collections for failure to pay for a bill you didn't know you had with them!!

The most HORRIFIC experience after being a loyal customer for many years . Worst customer service ever, today, October 27th at 1:15pm (Easter time) was threated  by the manager Joe or Josh with the employee ID # 1266129. I reported the same bad signal issue that I have been having a for a long time and he ended up telling me that I should pay more that I was not legible for a corporate discount and never gave me a solution. I tried to call again but it looks like he is the only manager working today at this shift. HELP!!! #HELPME Anybody knows who can help me with the signal issue? Also I would like to report this manager before I change to another phone company.

Purchased a prepaid unlimited service. Same day had no internet access an calls were terrible was unable to call long distance& couldnt hear calles. They stated 2 days the towers were down. Called & went back to cancel my service. An ported out my number out 4 days later. Do not use this service you will regret it. They refused to issue a credit to my account. Basically tmobile took my money& did not provide a service. Wish I had read the re iews before doing business.

edits.text: I’d. give a blank 0 if I could The worse service I ever had. Their network is not dependable I have nothing to do with a tower being down. Pay for a service with no results ! Garbage service

text: Trust me, if I could give less than 1 star I would! Let’s begin by stating that T Mobile outsources to the 3rd world country of the Philippines and there’s where the trouble starts…the dialect language barrier. Now add to it the abysmal phone connection. I’ve been trying for DAYS to bring our son’s phone over (from Verizon) to our family plan, after his divorce and once again this morning is another FAILED call, DROPPED call….personally I believe they merely hang up! I know they disclose at the beginning of the call that it’s recorded and I really hope they ALL were because my contact alone could be a teaching moment. Should it take DAYS to port over a number? Yes, I am in day 4 of attempting to port over one phone!! If any other company ran their customer service like this, they’d be gone!!!

Yesterday, October 27th 2021, I went to T-Mobile on Hull Street store number 637D, and to my surprise I was helped by a young lady by the name of ABENI PAGE. She went above and beyond to help me with a situation pertaining to my phone. I have been to several other locations of T-Mobile in the Hampton roads area. All of the representatives were unable to help me with my situation. Ms. ABIEN PAGE, without hesitation knew exactly what she was doing! She was knowledgeable and very helpful and able to multitask as well not to ignore any other customers in the store. ABENI PAGE, is deserving of a pay increase. She is an essential employee of T-Mobile and should be recognized. She restored my faith in T-Mobile. Perhaps she can offer something more to T-Mobile, if every representative was trained to obtain her qualities, there would be no negative posts about T-Mobile. I was so impressed with this young lady that I'm not stopping here! I will take time to personally contact Isaiah Johnson so he is well aware of a fabulous employee that's representing T-Mobile! Thank you ABENI PAGE for your time, for your assistance and restoring faith in my choosing T-Mobile and leaving Verizon.

I don't like anything about T-mobile.  Their website is not helpful at all.  Sprints website was filled with helpful information, but the T-Mobile Website is bare bones.  

They were sending me weekly Tuesday freebees or discounts offered by T-mobile but I no longer receive them.  

When I first switched from Sprint and bought a new android, the person who signed me up and ordered my phone was a manager at another location but it still took me 2 hours in the store to sign up for a new T-Mobile Plan and order a new phone.  I was the first one in and the last one out.  

When I finally received my new phone, it took another 2 hours in the store to transfer my data.  Once I got home I discovered that not all of my data transferred.  I finally figured out how to do that at home before I turned in my old phone.

Even trying to write this review my phone service is at two bars! My phone signal drops constantly. Now don’t get me wrong T-Mobile has some perks with joining them like discounts etc. but paying $54.68 a month for spotty service is a no for me. I’ll be switching to spectrum this Friday when I get paid again or heck maybe today. T-Mobile has been slowing my speeds since I first got with them and it’s awful. They are not the 5G kings they portray themselves to be. Not once have I ever gotten 5G on my 5G phone. How do you say you’re the top 5G carrier and can’t even keep your speeds steady or your customers with a signal? Not to mention the TWO DATA BREACHES THEY’ve had in less than a year from each other. Awful company

If I could give them 0 stars I would. Left them years ago and forced to return via Sprint. Sprint always took good care of our biz. Spent an hour on the phone last week, an hour in their re-seller store after,   where they refused to honor the pre-arranged phone upgrades, then an hour and 40 yesterday on phone again-all just to get 2 new phones. Mind boggling.

Horrible customer service. Got a new phone. Had trouble from the start. Finally after months of calling, they sent me a new phone, upgraded too for free. Write off the payments for the first. Apologized. THEY ARE CHARGING ME FOR THE UPGRADE!! No one calls back. Can't get anyone that is authorized to take $900 off my bill!!!
T Mobile is the worst Company! They have poor service. Worst customer service. They ruined Sprint when they bought it. I do not agree that they are the crown jewel of 5 G , or anything else.

Do not go to this phone company they are a complete scam they recently bought out my old phone provider and told me if I didn't switch to a 5G cell phone they were going to kick me in my entire family off of service we are extremely struggling so we went with the cheapest phones that were free and they were supposed to be $0 a month our bill has went up $40 because they lied in the phones are not free the phones are complete garbage texting and calling is extremely spotty and the phones will not connect to 5G or Wi-Fi I almost think they're doing the exact same thing AT&T did to their customers this company is a scam do not do business with them because they will not try to help you if you need it anybody on the call lines are extremely unhelpful and I wish that they would work with employees better so they can learn to speak to people properly on the phone whether they have an accent or not that has nothing to do with it it's a lack of caring from anybody involved with the business all they want is your money and to screw with your service

Terrible customer service, even worse than Sprint. They advertise prices on phones but they don't tell you those prices are only if you are trading in a phone. Pretty deceiving. Phone never has more than 3 bars for a signal. It is worth paying more for good service and honesty from other carriers.

Since their merging with Sprint, I had nothing but slow LTE problems!!!!
Aside from their bill kept skyrocketing, when your cell tower is full, they will move you to another cell tower, because that cell tower is full of users.. What a joke!!!!

They lie lie lie trying to fix a billing problem saying they will call back and don't the reps are a joke not all but everyone I dealt with f!@$ you tmobile I mean that if only I would have listened to the reviews in the first place go to he!! I won't be giving you another red cent you can bank on to

Went in because my phone stopoed getting emsils. The young nanvwas determined it was because my phone needed to be vacked up. After axweek of still no emails and backung up the phone, I received a text from a relative  indicating she had emailed me and it kicked back saying mailbox was fulk. I deleted emails and that solved my problem with no help from t mobile

I echo every one star review on this site. I have had the displeasure of dealing with T-Mobile in the past 2 weeks. It is a horrible experience. I never had to call Sprint. Not that Sprint was that great, but they weren't this bad.

An absolute nightmare...dishonest...impossible to reach Postpaid Customer Service post sale ...never again...!
For 2 days I have spent hours attempting to cancel a purchase which when I read the fine print will cost me dearly...I was advised to dispute any charges with my bank..which I did ...2 days later I received an Email saying my unwanted purchase was shipped...spent more wasted time attempting to resolve this nightmare😞
Never trust T Mobile Post Paid Reps...they will say anything to make a sale ...you will regret it..😠
Prepaid T Mobile is a whole different ballpark ...easy to reach agents, honest and ethical...

The support is god-awful. 3 phone calls for a total of 1 hour and 14 minutes just to buy a phone. They are very nice, but the way they explain things is horrible and every single thing takes forever. It sounds like they are running a call center from a public food market. You can't hear anything. They have been over-coached to be friendly, try to over-explain what they are doing, and apologize 100 times for every little thing.  Just do it. it's a phone, not open heart surgery.  I left AT&T for this.  AT&T has tremendous service, and I am already starting to regret the move.  I have a sinking feeling I'm going to be buying my way out of this contract soon.
You will wait hours on a customer service rep. Business accounts don’t get better treatment either.

T-mobile is misleading, intentionally confusing, and did not deliver on the savings promised. I spent hours on the phone with a T-Mobile agent working through the details of a new phone and plan. In the end, they did not honor the package I bought, which will cost me +$400 for the phone and another +$450 for the plan. Unbelievable how complicated they make the transaction just so it isn’t easy to know what you are buying. The offer looked great, but the real cost is a bait and switch. Do not trust this company!!!

Sprint/T-Mobile: I just wish that I could give them a zero rating. This company is a total bunch of frauds. I'm not even sure if what they do is legal. They changed my plan without my permission, lied to me into buying a phone, saying that I could use the phone on my old plan. After returning the phone they refused to refund my money. 
I honestly spent over 12 hours on the phone and on the Sprint chat trying o sort this out!
Never ever use this company!!
They are totally unethical!

We are an elderly couple on fixed income. We had Sprint and had excellent experience. We got text message about T-Mobile takeover. Told to come in to get a new SIM card.  Went, SIM card did not work in our phones had to go over to T-Mobile and the experience started as miserable and has remained so. They make promises about costs that are not true. I have spent hours on phone trying to rectify situation to no avail.  T-Mobile is by far the worst carrier we have ever worked with. DO NOT CONVERT TO THEM. They do not keep billing promises and find ways to keep adding on charges. They promised our costs as $80.00 month and have gone over $100.00 each month.  My husband and I are saving money to buy out the phones and switch.

I remember well when T-Mobile stole my credit card information and signed me up for their so-called service. After the 3rd time I went to their local office and told them I would sue them because of my PTSD, they immediately nixed all my charges.

I was with T-Mobile for a long time I lost my phone I called and reported it and filed a claim in August is now October they're still taking payments out of my checking account I close my account and they still got to it due to misunderstanding at my bank I still don't have a phone and now the password has been changed now Monday I've got to go to the bank and file a paperwork asking why they were allowed to still continue taking out of my checking account from August to now when I've never received my phone and the man at the store was so rude

Please be cautious dealing with customer representatives or expert they say something & your monthly statement is 100% opposite to what they promise , you have to explain them for five hours what was the deal ,& after fighting so long you will get the credit but this not the least . They will give you a good deal with trading value but at the the time of closure their call will be hang up & again the whole communications goes in different boat & result will be same they will put you on hold 1 hour 35 minutes 36 seconds & after that you have to cut the call , so my personal advice is to check your monthly statements & do not rely what they are promising. I am still struggling from 7.00 pm to 11.36 pm

Tmobile has excellent service but they probably seem bad to all of you guys because they are probably vaccinated. Don’t get the vaccinated guys. Or you guys are probably vaccinated and have gone absolutely bonkers/silly.

We switched from AT&T to T-mobile 6 months ago. The sales rep quoted a rate much better than we had as well as two new iPones at no cost or contract requirements. All was good for the first month. Then our bill showed that we needed to pay full price for one of the phones as they were unable to find one of the phones we had turned in. It to the next three months and numerous calls to lower the bill. 

Recently I purchased a new iPad with a plan that I’d receive a $200 rebate if we got a data plan for it. We did and submitted all of the paperwork. That and two subsequent submissions were denied for a reason that we could not understand. Two calls to their representatives told me that the issues were resolved only to have that be lie as well.

Worst customer service ever. They lie to us directly and assure they are not. Before they hang up the only thing they care about is that you will leave a 5 star rating for them. Really!

We’re leaving as soon as we can. T-mobile, never again.

The cellular service is absolutely terrible, you basically always have 1 bar of signal, and even when you have more, it’s not even that fast. I used to be with Verizon, and switching was a terrible idea. The only benefit of T-mobile is the price, but you get mediocre service.

I was trying to unlock my T-Mobile iPhone 7 Plus but they said that it might take up to 2 weeks and I called them almost 10 times. I was not satisfied with T-Mobile and its coverage as well. That's also why I am moving to Verizon.

reserved a vehicle through tmobile travel and what a mistake that was...we reserved a vehicle for pick up at cancun airport but when we arrived there was no rental agency called Nu car rental anywhere in the airport nor did they have a shuttle there to pick us up. I rented on the tmobile travel site specifically to have one there so as to not to have to figure out how to get to resort from airport. Called T-Mobile travel number and asked where was a car and was told you have to take a shuttle to their place they are not located as they lead us to believe at the airport which there are penty of car rentals there. So T-Mobile was no help on the trip but going through them was only a fiasco....never again
Worse company ever 😡😡I’m with this company for the last two years and half 😡😡😡

I'm only giving 1 star because I can't give zero. Where do I begin? The abbreviated version (since this took 2 months to accomplish, 15 phone calls, 10 visits to multiple stores one of which is 45 min away and many countless hours on the phone or sitting on hold for an average of 90 min each phone call) is this. No one knows what they are doing. I only wanted to close my account, pay off my phones and get my phones unlocked. My account was set up incorrectly, my sons phone was put on as a loan not a promotion so I'm paying full price for that, and I was lied to at how much the phones actually cost ( free vs $1000 is a big difference) It shouldn't have taken 60+ days to accomplish this. I was told by the store I had to call customer service. Then customer service tells me I have to deal with this in the store. Every single time I called customer service it was a 90-120 min phone call and then the call would be dropped. I'd have to call back because they never called me back. I would be promised that I'd get a follow up phone call in 48 hours and a week would go by without a call. I was advised by the store to give false information (that was illegal and fraudulent) to customer service to remedy my situation. Everyone (store and customer service) promised they would fix my issue and no one ever did. Until my very last phone call 63 days into the mess they created when I had to threaten to get my attorney involved. Magically my phones were unlocked and my bill was straightened out within 20 min of the call. I have was with sprint for 17 yrs prior to this and never had this kind of trouble with them. I'm with T-Mobile for less than a week and its a hot mess. I've never been so happy to get away from this company. This seems to be a common thread with a lot of customers and I should have read the reviews before changing to them. It would have saved me many hours, a lot of stress and thousands of dollars.
I left the one because they wouldn’t let me leave a zero y’all suck and cannot help your customers
